author: codex-agent

patch.value:
Brainstormed design for a first-class `pm plan` command optimized for agent harness planning loops.

Problem framing:
- Modern harnesses already expose an ephemeral plan surface: Codex update_plan, Claude Code task/todo panels, Cursor-style planning/checklist flows. Those are useful in-session but weak as durable project memory.
- `pm plan` should bridge that gap: keep the familiar lightweight plan/update loop while storing enough structured links to make plans searchable, resumable, auditable, and connected to pm items.
- The command should not replace Epics/Features/Tasks. It should be a progressive-disclosure planning artifact that can later materialize, link, or update pm items.

Core product idea:
- `pm plan create` starts a plan attached to a scope item or ad-hoc request, with optional `--parent`, `--related`, `--blocks`, `--blocked-by`, `--from-search`, and `--claim` helpers.
- `pm plan add-step` records ordered steps with `pending|in_progress|completed|blocked|skipped` status, owner, confidence, evidence, and optional linked pm IDs.
- `pm plan update-step` mirrors agent harness plan updates: exactly one active step by default, deterministic reorder/replace semantics, and append-only history entries.
- `pm plan link` attaches dependencies or related work after discovery without forcing item creation.
- `pm plan materialize` can turn selected plan steps into pm tasks/features while preserving links back to the source plan.
- `pm plan show` defaults to brief TOON, with `--depth standard|deep`, `--fields`, `--json`, and `--for-agent` projection modes.

Agent optimization principles:
- Low-token first: default output should show plan id, scope, current step, blocked steps, linked item IDs, and next command hints; deep detail should be opt-in.
- Harness-compatible vocabulary: create/update/complete/block steps map directly to Codex update_plan, Claude TodoWrite/TaskCreate, and Cursor plan checklist behavior.
- Dependency-first flow: every step may link `depends_on`, `related`, `blocks`, `implements`, `verifies`, or `discovered_from` pm IDs.
- Search-native storage: plan title, summary, step text, linked item titles, evidence comments, and decisions should enter the keyword/semantic corpus.
- Progressive disclosure: plans can begin as lightweight local session checklists and later gain item links, acceptance criteria, files/tests/docs, or materialized children.
- Safe automation: every mutation writes history; plan close requires explicit resolution/evidence; linked test/file/doc mutation boundaries remain existing pm commands.

Open design questions:
- Storage model: dedicated `plans/pm-plan-*.toon` artifacts versus plan sections embedded on a normal pm item. Dedicated artifacts are cleaner for many ephemeral plans; embedded plans are simpler and inherit item lifecycle.
- ID model: separate `pl-xxxx` ids versus normal pm item IDs. Separate ids avoid polluting task lists; normal items are easier for existing search/list/deps.
- Status semantics: whether a completed plan means the plan was executed, or only that the plan was handed off/materialized.
- UI contract: whether `pm plan` should emit direct harness adapter hints or stay harness-neutral and expose stable JSON for plugins/MCP to translate.

Recommended MVP:
1. Implement `pm plan create|show|update|add-step|complete-step|block-step|link|close` as a core command family with TOON/JSON output.
2. Store plans as a typed metadata section on a pm item first, because it reuses existing history, search, ownership, and dependency machinery.
3. Add `pm plan create --scope <item-id>` and `pm plan link --step <n> --related <id>` so agents can attach discovered context without creating duplicate tasks.
4. Add `pm plan materialize --steps 2,4 --type Task --parent <id>` after the basic loop is stable.
5. Add SDK/MCP contracts and plugin commands for Codex/Claude once the CLI contract is stable.
Add agent-optimized pm plan command with linked dependencies

Design and implement a first-class pm plan workflow that mirrors common agent harness planning surfaces while storing structured pm item links, dependency context, and searchable progressive-disclosure plan state.

Agents can create and update durable plans with ordered step statuses; plan steps can link parent/related/blocks/blocked-by/discovered-from pm items; output defaults to low-token TOON with JSON and field projections; plan data is searchable and history-backed; SDK/MCP/plugin contracts expose the workflow; implementation is covered by focused tests and isolated dogfood.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Duplicate-check evidence: ran pm context --limit 10; pm search 'pm plan agent planning dependencies related items progressive disclosure' --limit 10; pm search 'pm plan planner planning update_plan claude cursor codex dependencies' --limit 20; pm list-open --fields id,title,status,type,priority,parent,tags --limit 50; pm list-in-progress --limit 20. No existing first-class pm plan command feature was found. Existing pm-planner plugin docs and Codex plugin work are related but not duplicates.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Brainstorming source: user requested a pm plan feature comparable to Codex/Claude/Cursor plan surfaces,with easy dependency and related-item linking for agents.

message: Create feature item for agent-optimized pm plan design

value.note: existing Claude slash-command planning loop reference

value.author: codex-agent
value.text:
Concrete command sketch for `pm plan`:
- `pm plan create --title "..." --scope pm-xxxx --related pm-yyyy --claim --json` creates a durable plan attached to an item or request.
- `pm plan add-step <PLAN> --text "..." --depends-on pm-xxxx --related pm-yyyy --status pending` records a linked step.
- `pm plan update-step <PLAN> 2 --status in_progress --evidence "..."` should enforce a single active step unless `--allow-multiple-active` is passed.
- `pm plan complete-step <PLAN> 2 --evidence "tests passed"` is the harness-equivalent checklist completion mutation.
- `pm plan show <PLAN> --depth brief|standard|deep --fields id,current_step,linked_items --json` gives progressive-disclosure reads.
- `pm plan materialize <PLAN> --steps 2,3 --type Task --parent pm-xxxx` converts selected steps into real pm items and links them back with discovered_from/implements relationships.

Suggested JSON shape: `{ id, scope_item_id, title, status, current_step_id, steps:[{id, order, text, status, linked_items:[{id, kind}], evidence, updated_at}], summary:{pending,active,blocked,completed}, next_actions:[...] }`.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Existing `pm-planner` plugin assets are workflow guidance, not a durable first-class plan artifact. The durable gap is a core/SDK/MCP plan state model that agents can update like harness checklists while linking each step to pm dependencies and related work. Keep the MVP harness-neutral and expose stable JSON so Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, and plugins can adapt it without hardcoding one harness model into core.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Evidence: brainstorming captured in pm-v7dj body/notes/learnings; related items linked: parent pm-rnpb, related pm-0c9q, pm-b7sd, pm-59gj; existing planner docs linked via pm docs. Bootstrap passed: npm install -g ., pm --version 2026.5.14, node v25.9.0, pnpm 10.33.0, pnpm build. No code changes were made.

patch.value:
Brainstormed design for a first-class `pm plan` command optimized for agent harness planning loops.

Problem framing:
- Modern harnesses already expose an ephemeral plan surface: Codex update_plan, Claude Code task/todo panels, Cursor-style planning/checklist flows. Those are useful in-session but weak as durable project memory.
- `pm plan` should bridge that gap: keep the familiar lightweight plan/update loop while storing enough structured links to make plans searchable, resumable, auditable, and connected to pm items.
- The command should not replace Epics/Features/Tasks. It should be a progressive-disclosure planning artifact that can later materialize, link, or update pm items.

Core product idea:
- `pm plan create` starts a plan attached to a scope item or ad-hoc request, with optional `--parent`, `--related`, `--blocks`, `--blocked-by`, `--from-search`, and `--claim` helpers.
- `pm plan add-step` records ordered steps with `pending|in_progress|completed|blocked|skipped` status, owner, confidence, evidence, and optional linked pm IDs.
- `pm plan update-step` mirrors agent harness plan updates: exactly one active step by default, deterministic reorder/replace semantics, and append-only history entries.
- `pm plan link` attaches dependencies or related work after discovery without forcing item creation.
- `pm plan materialize` can turn selected plan steps into pm tasks/features while preserving links back to the source plan.
- `pm plan show` defaults to brief TOON, with `--depth standard|deep`, `--fields`, `--json`, and `--for-agent` projection modes.

Agent optimization principles:
- Low-token first: default output should show plan id, scope, current step, blocked steps, linked item IDs, and next command hints; deep detail should be opt-in.
- Harness-compatible vocabulary: create/update/complete/block steps map directly to Codex update_plan, Claude TodoWrite/TaskCreate, and Cursor plan checklist behavior.
- Dependency-first flow: every step may link `depends_on`, `related`, `blocks`, `implements`, `verifies`, or `discovered_from` pm IDs.
- Search-native storage: plan title, summary, step text, linked item titles, evidence comments, and decisions should enter the keyword/semantic corpus.
- Progressive disclosure: plans can begin as lightweight local session checklists and later gain item links, acceptance criteria, files/tests/docs, or materialized children.
- Safe automation: every mutation writes history; plan close requires explicit resolution/evidence; linked test/file/doc mutation boundaries remain existing pm commands.

Open design questions:
- Storage model: dedicated `plans/pm-plan-*.toon` artifacts versus plan sections embedded on a normal pm item. Dedicated artifacts are cleaner for many ephemeral plans; embedded plans are simpler and inherit item lifecycle.
- ID model: separate `pl-xxxx` ids versus normal pm item IDs. Separate ids avoid polluting task lists; normal items are easier for existing search/list/deps.
- Status semantics: whether a completed plan means the plan was executed, or only that the plan was handed off/materialized.
- UI contract: whether `pm plan` should emit direct harness adapter hints or stay harness-neutral and expose stable JSON for plugins/MCP to translate.

Recommended MVP:
1. Implement `pm plan create|show|update|add-step|complete-step|block-step|link|close` as a core command family with TOON/JSON output.
2. Store plans as a typed metadata section on a pm item first, because it reuses existing history, search, ownership, and dependency machinery.
3. Add `pm plan create --scope <item-id>` and `pm plan link --step <n> --related <id>` so agents can attach discovered context without creating duplicate tasks.
4. Add `pm plan materialize --steps 2,4 --type Task --parent <id>` after the basic loop is stable.
5. Add SDK/MCP contracts and plugin commands for Codex/Claude once the CLI contract is stable.

## 2026-05-17 Research Update: Make plans a first-class `Plan` item type

Decision: implement plans as a normal built-in pm item type named `Plan`, with lowercase `plan` accepted through the existing case-insensitive type resolver. This is better than a separate `plans/` artifact model because it reuses item storage, search, dependencies, ownership, comments, notes, learnings, linked files/docs/tests, validation, and history streams immediately.

Current repo facts for implementers:
- Built-in item types currently live in `src/types.ts` as `BUILTIN_ITEM_TYPE_VALUES`.
- Built-in type folder mapping currently lives in `src/core/shared/constants.ts` as `TYPE_TO_FOLDER`.
- The runtime type registry already resolves names case-insensitively through `alias_to_type`, so adding `Plan` should make `--type plan` work without a separate lowercase built-in.
- Settings and extensions can already add item types, but this feature should be core because agent plan mode is a first-class pm workflow, not a package-only optional workflow.
- `pm contracts --command create --flags-only --json` currently reports no `Plan` type, so implementation must update contracts/help/completion/runtime schema tests.

Online research summary:
- Codex guidance for long-running work favors durable living execution plans that are self-contained, include progress, decision logs, validation, and enough context for a stateless future agent to resume.
- Claude Code Plan Mode is read-only before approval: it reads files and proposes a plan without writing changes until the user approves. Its emerging Task tools split list creation and updates into `TaskCreate`, `TaskUpdate`, `TaskList`, and `TaskGet`, with statuses `pending`, `in_progress`, and `completed`, plus blocked-by/blocks and owner metadata.
- Cursor Plan Mode researches the codebase, asks clarifying questions, creates editable plans with file paths and code references, supports creating/updating plans, and can save plans as Markdown for future reference. Cursor's agent best-practices guide says planning before coding is the most impactful workflow change and recommends saving plans to make future agent resumption easier.

Source links used for the research:
- OpenAI Codex PLANS.md / ExecPlan cookbook: https://developers.openai.com/cookbook/articles/codex_exec_plans
- OpenAI Codex CLI overview: https://developers.openai.com/codex/cli
- Claude Code common workflows / Plan Mode: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/common-workflows
- Claude Agent SDK todo/task tracking: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/todo-tracking
- Cursor Plan Mode announcement: https://cursor.com/blog/plan-mode
- Cursor agent best practices: https://cursor.com/blog/agent-best-practices

Required `Plan` item metadata shape:
- `plan_mode`: `draft|research|review|approved|executing|paused|completed|superseded` for plan-specific lifecycle while retaining normal item `status`.
- `plan_scope`: short statement of the target change or investigation.
- `plan_harness`: optional `codex|claude-code|cursor|generic` provenance.
- `plan_steps`: ordered list of stable step ids with `title`, `body`, `status`, `owner`, `evidence`, `linked_items`, `files`, `tests`, `docs`, `created_at`, `updated_at`.
- `plan_decisions`: append-only decision log entries with decision, rationale, source/evidence, author, timestamp.
- `plan_discoveries`: surprises, risks, unknowns, and codebase findings discovered during read-only planning.
- `plan_validation`: expected commands, manual checks, and observable outcomes.
- `plan_resume_context`: compact summary for a future agent after context compaction or a new session.

Step status model:
- Support `pending`, `in_progress`, `completed`, `blocked`, `skipped`, and `superseded`.
- Default invariant: one `in_progress` step per Plan item unless `--allow-multiple-active` is explicit for parallel work.
- Preserve every step mutation through normal pm history entries. Do not rewrite steps in-place outside pm mutation commands.

Dependency and relation model:
- Use existing pm dependencies for item-level relations: `parent`, `child`, `related`, `blocks`, `discovered_from`.
- Add step-level `linked_items` entries with `id`, `kind`, `note`, and optional `required_before_step` boolean.
- `pm plan link <PLAN> --step <id|order> --related pm-xxxx` should add a step-level link and optionally a top-level item dependency when `--promote-to-item-dep` is passed.
- `pm plan materialize` should create normal Tasks/Features from selected steps and add bidirectional `discovered_from`/`child` or `related` links.

Command MVP:
- `pm create --type Plan --title ...` should work as a normal item create path.
- `pm plan create --title ... --scope ... --parent pm-xxxx --related pm-yyyy --from-search "query"` should create a `Plan` item with duplicate-check/search evidence.
- `pm plan show <ID> --depth brief|standard|deep --fields ... --json` should provide progressive disclosure. Brief should show id, title, plan_mode, current step, blockers, linked pm ids, and next suggested command.
- `pm plan add-step <ID> --title ... --body ... --depends-on pm-xxxx --related pm-yyyy` should append a stable step id.
- `pm plan update-step <ID> <STEP> --status ... --evidence ...` should patch one step and history-log the mutation.
- `pm plan complete-step`, `pm plan block-step`, `pm plan reorder-step`, and `pm plan materialize` should be convenience wrappers around the same typed mutation path.
- SDK/MCP contracts should expose both normal item CRUD support for type `Plan` and dedicated plan actions for harness-native integrations.

Output requirements:
- Default TOON output must be low-token and agent-readable.
- JSON output must include deterministic fields: `item`, `plan`, `current_step`, `steps_summary`, `linked_items`, `next_actions`, and `history_event` or mutation summary.
- `--fields` must support low-token projections such as `id,title,plan_mode,current_step,blocked_steps,linked_items`.
- Search corpus must include plan scope, step titles/bodies, decisions, discoveries, validation, dependencies, and linked docs/files/tests.

Safety and history requirements:
- Plan items must use the same history hash-chain behavior as all other pm items.
- Every step add/update/reorder/link/materialize mutation must produce a history entry.
- Read-only planning should be representable without code edits: `plan_mode=research|review` and item `status=open|in_progress` are enough.
- Approval to execute should be explicit: `pm plan approve <ID>` can set `plan_mode=approved`, while normal implementation claims/status continue through existing item workflow.
- Closing a Plan item should require evidence of one of: implemented/materialized, superseded by another plan/item, canceled with rationale, or completed as analysis-only.

Implementation touchpoints:
- `src/types.ts`: add `Plan` to `BUILTIN_ITEM_TYPE_VALUES`.
- `src/core/shared/constants.ts`: map `Plan` to a `plans` folder and include folder creation/defaults where required.
- `src/core/item/type-registry.ts`: confirm lowercase alias behavior and add tests that `--type plan` resolves to `Plan`.
- `src/core/item/item-format.ts` and `src/types.ts`: add typed plan metadata fields if the project chooses strong typing over generic metadata.
- `src/core/search/corpus.ts` and search command tests: include plan fields in keyword/semantic corpus.
- `src/cli/commands/create.ts`, `update.ts`, and help/usage surfaces: ensure `Plan` participates in create/update validation without schedule presets.
- New `src/cli/commands/plan.ts`: dedicated step lifecycle commands.
- `src/cli/main.ts`, `src/cli/commands/index.ts`, completion, help, contracts, SDK and MCP surfaces: register/discover `pm plan`.
- Plugins `plugins/pm-claude` and `plugins/pm-codex`: add slash commands/skills once core contracts exist.
- `scripts/dogfood-package-first.mjs`: add Plan create/show/update/link/materialize smoke coverage.

Recommended child implementation order:
1. Add built-in `Plan` item type and folder/runtime/schema/search integration.
2. Add `pm plan` command family with typed step lifecycle mutations and history tests.
3. Add SDK/MCP/plugin/docs/dogfood coverage and agent-harness examples.

author: codex-agent

patch.value: Built-in Plan item type exists and --type plan resolves to Plan; Plan items are stored in normal pm item storage with full history hash-chain tracking; plan metadata captures scope, harness provenance, ordered steps, step links, decisions, discoveries, validation, and resume context; pm plan create/show/add-step/update-step/complete-step/block-step/link/materialize/approve expose agent-optimized workflows; steps can link pm dependencies and related items; outputs default to low-token TOON with JSON/fields/depth projections; search indexes Plan scope/steps/decisions/discoveries/dependencies; SDK/MCP/plugins/docs/dogfood cover Codex/Claude/Cursor-style usage; focused tests and isolated dogfood prove behavior.

Design and implement a first-class built-in Plan item type plus pm plan command family that mirrors Codex, Claude Code, and Cursor planning surfaces while reusing pm item history, search, dependencies, ownership, and progressive-disclosure outputs.

message: Refine pm plan design toward built-in Plan item type

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Research and planning evidence 2026-05-17: online sources reviewed for Codex ExecPlans, Codex CLI, Claude Plan Mode/Task tools, Cursor Plan Mode, and Cursor agent best practices. Direction changed from sidecar plan artifact to built-in Plan item type that reuses pm history/search/dependencies. Created child implementation tasks pm-jauk, pm-ze5g, and pm-aqat with linked files/tests and acceptance criteria for future agents.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Parent Feature moved to in_progress while Claude Code agent works on child implementation pm-jauk (Plan item type) → pm-ze5g (pm plan commands) → pm-aqat (SDK/MCP/plugin/dogfood).

patch.value: Parent Feature closed after all three child tasks (pm-jauk, pm-ze5g, pm-aqat) implemented and verified: Plan built-in type + storage + search corpus, pm plan command family with full step lifecycle, and SDK/MCP/plugins/docs/dogfood exposure. 1355 tests passing at 100% coverage; release Sentry/telemetry gates green; dogfood-package-first.mjs ok=true.
First-class agent-optimized pm plan command + Plan item type shipped with full lifecycle, history hash chain, search corpus, SDK/MCP/plugin/docs exposure, and dogfood verification.
Codex/Claude/Cursor-style planning workflows are first-class in pm with durable, resumable, searchable plans.
Verified: 1355 tests pass at 100% coverage; pm plan + pm_plan MCP exposes 16 subcommands; dogfood-package-first.mjs ok=true; Sentry critical=0/high=0; telemetry finish_error_rate_pct=2.06.
